title: Daily Quote
description: One quote a day, with a Wikiquote link to the author when one exists

items.title: “Everyone has talent. What's rare is the courage to follow it to the dark places where it leads.” — Erica Jong
items.content_text: Everyone has talent. What's rare is the courage to follow it to the dark places where it leads. — Erica Jong

items.title: “Prepare for the worst, expect the best and take what comes. This is fatalism.” — Anonymous
items.content_text: Prepare for the worst, expect the best and take what comes. This is fatalism. — Anonymous

items.title: “Who do you think's better looking people in the north or people in the south?” — Umar ibn Al-Khattāb (R.A)
items.content_text: Who do you think's better looking people in the north or people in the south? — Umar ibn Al-Khattāb (R.A)

items.title: “All the good are friends of one another.” — Zeno of Citium
items.content_text: All the good are friends of one another. — Zeno of Citium

items.title: “Be sure you put your feet in the right place, then stand firm.” — Abraham Lincoln
items.content_text: Be sure you put your feet in the right place, then stand firm. — Abraham Lincoln

items.title: “There are three signs of a knowledgeable person: knowledge, forbearance and silence.” — Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A)
items.content_text: There are three signs of a knowledgeable person: knowledge, forbearance and silence. — Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A)

items.title: “Human beings are makers, usually of a mountain out of a molehill.” — Mokokoma Mokhonoana
items.content_text: Human beings are makers, usually of a mountain out of a molehill. — Mokokoma Mokhonoana

items.title: “The injuries we do and those we suffer are seldom weighed in the same scales.” — Aesop
items.content_text: The injuries we do and those we suffer are seldom weighed in the same scales. — Aesop

items.title: “The element of the unexpected and the unforeseeable is what gives some of its relish to life and saves us from falling into the mechanical thralldom of the logicians.” — Winston Churchill
items.content_text: The element of the unexpected and the unforeseeable is what gives some of its relish to life and saves us from falling into the mechanical thralldom of the logicians. — Winston Churchill

items.title: “If you wish to know the mind of a man, listen to his words.” — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
items.content_text: If you wish to know the mind of a man, listen to his words. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

items.title: “Sadness may be part of life but there is no need to let it dominate your entire life.” — Byron Pulsifer
items.content_text: Sadness may be part of life but there is no need to let it dominate your entire life. — Byron Pulsifer

items.title: “The absent are never without fault, nor the present without excuse.” — Benjamin Franklin
items.content_text: The absent are never without fault, nor the present without excuse. — Benjamin Franklin

items.title: “Our greatest glory is not in never failing but rising everytime we fall.” — Confucius
items.content_text: Our greatest glory is not in never failing but rising everytime we fall. — Confucius

items.title: “Fame always goes with the principles.” — Baltasar Gracián
items.content_text: Fame always goes with the principles. — Baltasar Gracián

items.title: “Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps, down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision.” — Ayn Rand
items.content_text: Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps, down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision. — Ayn Rand

items.title: “Tolerance starts when you practise it.” — Proverb
items.content_text: Tolerance starts when you practise it. — Proverb

items.title: “The man who spends his time choosing one resort after another in a hunt for peace and quiet, will in every place he visits find something to prevent him from relaxing. The story is told that someone complained to Socrates that travelling abroad had never done him any good and received the reply: "What else can you expect, seeing that you always take yourself along with you when you go abroad?‟” — Seneca
items.content_text: The man who spends his time choosing one resort after another in a hunt for peace and quiet, will in every place he visits find something to prevent him from relaxing. The story is told that someone complained to Socrates that travelling abroad had never done him any good and received the reply: "What else can you expect, seeing that you always take yourself along with you when you go abroad?‟ — Seneca

items.title: “I want to sing like the birds sing, not worrying about who hears or what they think.” — Rumi
items.content_text: I want to sing like the birds sing, not worrying about who hears or what they think. — Rumi

items.title: “War is not an adventure. It is a disease. It is like typhus.” — Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
items.content_text: War is not an adventure. It is a disease. It is like typhus. — Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

items.title: “In all of us, even in good men, there is a lawless wild-beast nature, which peers out in sleep” — Sokrates
items.content_text: In all of us, even in good men, there is a lawless wild-beast nature, which peers out in sleep — Sokrates

items.title: “Every person is a fool in somebody's opinion.” — Danish Proverb
items.content_text: Every person is a fool in somebody's opinion. — Danish Proverb

items.title: “Religions, which condemn the pleasures of sense, drive men to seek the pleasures of power. Throughout history power has been the vice of the ascetic.” — Bertrand Russell
items.content_text: Religions, which condemn the pleasures of sense, drive men to seek the pleasures of power. Throughout history power has been the vice of the ascetic. — Bertrand Russell
